# Mechanical Earth Model

**Summary**: A quantitative model used to determine in-situ stresses and rock mechanical properties.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] constructed a mechanical-earth-model (MEM) to evaluate wellbore stability and fracture orientation.

## Scientific Context

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] Essential for predicting wellbore stability and optimizing hydraulic fracturing design.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] Integration of log data, core analysis, and geomechanical parameters.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The MEM integrates information from geology, rock mechanics, geophysics, and petrophysics. ([[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016, p. 65]])
